price_details: site doesn't post numbers but describes tiered packages, the top one is literally called the Hot Fudge Sundae and includes two photographers, a one hour engagement session, and a custom printed album with full printing rights so no per-image fees later
notes:
-4.8 stars over 119 reviews so a real track record here
-'straightforward pricing with everything included, no surprise fees' per the site, you just have to inquire to see actual numbers
-a review from a Manor House wedding said having lead + second shooter meant nothing got missed, they caught both partners getting ready at the same time
-covers weddings/elopements/proposals across CO, travel included within an hour of their Denver studio and they'll go further into the high country for the right couple

price_details: husband and wife team (Stacy and Trent), documentary style covering weddings and elopements, serve Breck, Telluride, Vail, and basically anywhere in CO plus destination weddings worldwide, exact package breakdown not on the pricing digest so ask them directly
notes: A trade group listing pegs them starting around $6,200 and they shoot documentary style as a husband and wife team out of Silverthorne. One review described Trent driving four hours to Moab last minute after another photographer broke his hand a week before the wedding, then shooting the entire day solo. A different couple said he coordinated a ski lap with family and friends the morning before their wedding when plans changed due to weather. Reviews consistently mention him staying late and going well past what was expected.

price_details:
- a la carte destination engagement add-on is $800, discounted if bundled with a wedding package
- top package (Quaking Aspen) had 2 photographers, 2 videographers, plus an assistant/content creator on one wedding mentioned on her instagram
- exact hours per tier aren't spelled out, you get a full quote after a call
notes:
-been shooting since 2012, mountain weddings w/ an estes park focus but does elopements too
-reviews keep saying she knows rocky mountain nat'l park inside and out & isn't afraid to hike for a shot
-a texas couple said she gave them a full walkthrough a week before their elopement so they knew what to expect
-one reviewer straight up said 'based on reviews and pricing, easily the best bang for our buck'

price_details: site says every event is priced custom, no packages listed, you book a video call or in person meetup to build a collection, worth asking directly about elopement vs full wedding rates
notes: tbh her site is all 'contact me for pricing' but the actual work covers weddings, elopements, and full adventure elopement/micro wedding planning, plus she also does corporate and maternity on the side. reviews keep saying she's on time and easy to work with, one reviewer said she's been shooting their family for 5-6 years and always delivers. another couple said she handled a chaotic day smoothly and made sure the important stuff got captured. no pricing numbers anywhere though so budget for a call before you know what you're working with

price_text: Most couples invest around $8k for full day coverage
price_details: collections are flexible/tailored rather than fixed tiers, custom pricing available for micro weddings, elopements, engagement sessions, and multi day celebrations, no add on breakdown published so you'd confirm specifics when you inquire
notes: Went down a review rabbit hole on this one. A couple said they first met Hannah while she was shooting a friend's wedding and loved her documentary approach before ever booking her themselves. Another review specifically called out that she's less about posed shots and more about capturing people actually living and celebrating, which is exactly the style she advertises on her site. A third reviewer said she has a way of making you feel at ease on your own wedding day, which came up more than once across the reviews I read.

price_details: site doesn't break out packages beyond the hourly starting rate, you talk through your day with her directly to build a plan
notes:
- Style: lifestyle portraits, weddings, elopements, adventure photography
- Loves an adventure engagement session, site literally asks if you want one on top of a mountain
- One review: shot a ceremony at the Jeffco courthouse then did couples shots at Red Rocks after
- Another reviewer said she's so unobtrusive during a shoot 'you barely even know she's there'

price_text: Party Hard / Keep It Simple / Elopement packages
price_details:
- Additional hours: $600/per
- Second photographer add on: 'Assistant Second Photographer'
- Super 8mm film add ons: wedding day (6+ min) $2,500, elopement (6+ min) $2,500, engagement (3 min) $800
- Travel: all fees are built into the package price for CO, for out of state she steers you to a different tier (Party Hard for western US, Whole Freakin' Weekend for eastern US/Canada/Mexico, Worldwide for Europe/South America)
- Extras: full black and white gallery add on $600, RAW image release fee $2,000
notes:
Review roundup:
- One bride said she heard about Heather from a friend's wedding first, researched other photographers, and still ended up booking her
- Multiple reviews use the word badass unprompted, one specifically about how she handled difficult family members without losing her cool
- 111 google reviews at 5 stars is a big enough sample that the consistency actually means something

price_details: micro wedding and elopement focused packages, 50% retainer due at booking applied to your final balance which is due day of, 4 week turnaround for full galleries but social sneak peeks usually land within 72hrs, travel fees kick in outside the Denver foothills so ask if your location qualifies
notes: Found this one through a review thread and it's clearly a micro wedding and elopement specialist rather than a full wedding shooter. One bride said she already rebooked Lisa for her actual wedding after loving the engagement shoot experience first, helped pick outfits and worked off a Pinterest board. Another review said she helped coordinate group photos start to finish on the wedding day itself. A couple who eloped in Conifer specifically called out the first look shots at Staunton State Park as a highlight.

price_text: starts at $2k for 4hrs, scales up to 12hrs
price_details:
- second photographer add on is $80/hr with a 4hr minimum
- 20 page handcrafted album is $850 extra
- travel fees are included if you're anywhere in the front range, winter park, dillon or nearby, so no surprise travel bill in that radius
notes:
-multiple reviews mention she meets couples twice before the wedding to build an actual timeline, not just showing up day of
-she reportedly scouts the venue ahead of time to plan shot locations in advance
-one review said everyone they've shown the photos to has audibly gasped, for what that's worth
-communication got called out specifically as 'on point' in more than one review

price_text: Weddings start at $5k, elopements start at $3k
price_details: site says inquire for full package details beyond the starting numbers, documentary style shooting out of Crested Butte
notes: Documentary style is the whole pitch here, and it fits given she's based in Crested Butte, which she calls the wildflower capital of Colorado on her site. A couple who eloped there said she was responsive without being pushy when they first reached out, then flexible once they booked. Another reviewer said they were nervous about being awkward in front of the camera and Jacey talked them through small adjustments until it felt natural. Worth noting all three reviews I found were about elopements specifically, less clear how a bigger wedding day would go with her.

price_details: site doesn't list numbers, copy describes a curated collection approach rather than packaged tiers, you'd inquire directly for a proposal
notes: Site markets itself as fine art rather than standard wedding photography, and the reviews back up an editorial, editing-heavy style. One review mentioned booking both an engagement and wedding shoot with her and specifically praised the editing style as a reason to come back for the wedding after already liking the engagement photos. She's based in Aspen but the reviews and site copy reference California, New York, and Europe as places she's shot, so this reads as a destination-first photographer rather than someone tied to one venue circuit.
